reason: MCQ; papers provided. The correct options (A, C, F) are solvable from the stem plus general mechanics: concentrated venting through a small orifice increases jet velocity/force and can entrain/tear solids; distributed venting lowers per-area force; and “more solid fraction at equal total mass loss” implies a lower gas-to-solid mass ratio. Incorrect options introduce unsupported alternative mechanisms (greater total gas volume, electrolyte differences, heat retention) and are not defensible given the stated identical capacity/comparable total mass and the mechanism focus. No contradiction with the evidence bundle, which explicitly attributes the effect to overpressure valve jetting and higher solid fraction.

Without those thresholds, multiple option labelings are defensible/ambiguous.

Closed-book solvability failure: Option C requires a paper-specific numeric slope (-0.42 K·l/Wh) that is not provided in the stem, so the 84 K calculation is not derivable from the admissible information set. Because C is labeled Correct, the item is inadmissible.
